summary.brief: Wadsworth v. Sharma. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: In 2006, doctors diagnosed Stephanie Wadsworth with Stage IIIC1 breast cancer in her left breast.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Wadsworth underwent a series of follow-up PET/CT scans on August 22, 2006, July 18, 2007, and August 28, 2008. Each scan was negative for metastatic disease.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Wadsworth underwent a new round of diagnostic testing ordered on March 15, 2013 by her oncologist, Poornima Sharma, M.D.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Wadsworth produced an abnormal PET/CT scan on April 1, 2013, depicting a new and potentially cancerous lesion on her clavicle.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Sharma reviewed the scan but did not report the results to Ms. Wadsworth or conduct further testing.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Wadsworth fell and injured her right shoulder. Ms. Wadsworth went to the hospital for her shoulder injury, and a bone scan depicted a malignant bone lesion on her right clavicle.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On March 8, 2016, Ms. Wadsworth underwent an open biopsy, which showed that the lesion was 'metastatic adenocarcinoma compatible with a breast primary.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Wadsworth continued treatment but passed away on June 10, 2017.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Schneider stated that 'there is no cure once you have metastasis' and that 'the average person like [Ms. Wadsworth] would have lived . . . an additional [eighty] months' from the date of the abnormal scan.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Schneider

evidence_index.text: Dr. Stark did not offer an opinion on whether treating Ms. Wadsworth sooner would have cured her breast cancer.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Stark

evidence_index.text: The circuit court granted the motion for summary judgment, finding that 'this is really a loss of chance cause of action, which does not exist in . . . Maryland.'
evidence_index.speaker: Circuit court

evidence_index.text: The Court of Special Appeals held that Mr. Wadsworth could not show that Dr. Sharma’s conduct caused Ms. Wadsworth’s death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court of Special Appeals

evidence_index.text: We hold that the Circuit Court for Baltimore County and the Court of Special Appeals correctly decided Mr. Wadsworth’s wrongful death claim because he pleaded a loss of chance case, which is not recognized in Maryland.
evidence_index.speaker: Court of Appeals

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Ms. Wadsworth was diagnosed with Stage IIIC1 breast cancer in her left breast in 2006. She underwent a left mastectomy, chemotherapy, and radiation therapy. Follow-up PET/CT scans in 2006, 2007, and 2008 were negative for metastatic disease. A new round of diagnostic testing was ordered on March 15, 2013, which included a PET/CT scan showing a potentially cancerous lesion on her clavicle.

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with history of breast cancer and abnormal PET/CT scan
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management after identifying a potentially cancerous lesion on the PET/CT scan?

options.description: Conduct further testing and inform the patient
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard practice to confirm diagnosis and inform patient for timely management

options.description: Do not report the results and conduct no further testing
options.clinical_reasoning: Neglects the need for timely intervention in potential metastatic disease

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not report the results or conduct further testing
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant stated that further action was not necessary at that point

expected_action_court.description: Further testing should have been conducted and results communicated to the patient
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established the need for timely follow-up in suspected metastatic disease

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to act on abnormal findings led to delayed diagnosis and management
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Timely follow-up testing and patient notification
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed metastatic breast cancer and passed away

key_findings: Loss of chance doctrine not recognized in Maryland
Plaintiff could not prove causation by a preponderance of the evidence

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to act on abnormal PET/CT scan
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Not applicable due to legal outcome
